indicators.name: no flat personal work surface
indicators.rationale: The absence of any desk or table at sitting height is the primary barrier to setting up a computer workstation.

indicators.name: upholstered dining chairs
indicators.rationale: Dining chairs are not suitable for extended computer work due to their lack of back support and ergonomic design.

indicators.name: high-traffic restaurant layout
indicators.rationale: The restaurant layout is designed for social dining, not focused solo work. The presence of multiple tables and chairs indicates a public space rather than a private workspace.

indicators.name: ambient chandelier overhead lighting
indicators.rationale: Decorative ambient lighting provides no task illumination for screen work and may cause screen glare.

indicators.name: decorative wall panels
indicators.rationale: Decorative wall panels suggest a more formal and less functional environment, which is not conducive to focused solo work.

reasoning_summary: This indoor scene is highly unsuitable for focused solo computer work. The lack of a flat personal work surface, ergonomic seating, and a high-traffic restaurant layout make it difficult to set up a productive workstation. The decorative lighting and wall panels further suggest a formal environment that is not conducive to focused solo work.
